name: Risk-Based Credit Decision
description: Guides credit analysts through a structured risk-based credit assessment — from applicant data collection through scoring, risk stratification, and approval/denial with conditions

defaultWeights.label: Default Risk
defaultWeights.description: Sensitivity to borrower default probability — high rejects marginal applicants, low approves more

defaultWeights.label: Processing Speed
defaultWeights.description: Application turnaround pressure — high auto-decides more, low requires manual review

defaultWeights.label: Regulatory Weight
defaultWeights.description: Compliance strictness — high applies conservative regulatory buffers, low uses minimum requirements

defaultWeights.label: Profit Margin
defaultWeights.description: Revenue optimization — high prices for maximum margin, low competes on rate

defaultWeights.label: Customer Retention
defaultWeights.description: Existing relationship value — high favors existing customers, low treats all applicants equally

defaultWeights.label: Portfolio Diversity
defaultWeights.description: Concentration risk management — high diversifies across sectors, low focuses on best returns

defaultWeights.label: Fraud Detection
defaultWeights.description: Anti-fraud sensitivity — high flags more for review, low streamlines approvals

defaultWeights.label: Collateral Weight
defaultWeights.description: Importance of collateral in decision — high requires strong backing, low considers cash flow

nodes.label: Applicant Type
nodes.description: What type of credit applicant is this?
nodes.prompt: Is this a consumer (individual) or commercial (business) credit application?

nodes.label: Credit Score Range
nodes.description: Applicant's credit score determines the initial risk tier
nodes.prompt: What is the applicant's credit score range?

nodes.label: Regulatory Compliance
nodes.assumption: This decision follows Fair Lending Act (ECOA), Truth in Lending Act (TILA), and applicable state regulations. All decisions must be documented with specific, non-discriminatory reasons for adverse actions.

nodes.label: Debt-to-Income Ratio
nodes.description: DTI is a key risk indicator for repayment ability
nodes.prompt: What is the applicant's debt-to-income ratio?

nodes.label: Initial Risk Tier
nodes.description: Classify into risk tier based on credit score + DTI
nodes.condition: Combine credit score range and DTI to determine initial risk classification

nodes.label: Collateral Available?
nodes.prompt: Is the applicant offering collateral to secure the credit?

nodes.label: Employment Stability
nodes.description: Length and stability of income source
nodes.prompt: How would you characterize the applicant's employment/income stability?

nodes.label: Enhanced Review Needed?
nodes.description: Determine if application needs manual underwriter review
nodes.condition: If risk tier is Medium-High or above, OR DTI > 36%, OR employment < 1 year, escalate to enhanced review

nodes.label: Fraud Screening Passed
nodes.description: Assumes identity verification and fraud screening are complete
nodes.assumption: Applicant has passed KYC (Know Your Customer) verification, OFAC screening, and automated fraud detection. Identity documents have been validated. If any fraud flags exist, this tree should not be used — escalate to fraud investigation team.

nodes.label: Requested Amount
nodes.description: Loan amount relative to income determines exposure
nodes.prompt: What is the requested credit amount relative to the applicant's annual income?

nodes.label: Risk-Based Pricing Tier
nodes.description: Determine interest rate tier based on aggregate risk factors
nodes.condition: Map combined risk profile (score + DTI + collateral + employment + amount) to pricing tier: Prime, Near-Prime, Subprime, or Decline

nodes.label: Approve — Prime Rate
nodes.description: Low-risk applicant: approve at prime interest rate
nodes.systemPrompt: You are a credit decisioning system. Generate a structured credit approval letter for a prime-rate applicant. Include: approved amount, interest rate range (prime), key terms, required documentation, and compliance disclosures required by TILA and ECOA.
nodes.template: Generate prime-rate credit approval for applicant type: {{applicantType}}, credit score: {{creditScoreRange}}, DTI: {{dtiRatio}}, collateral: {{hasCollateral}}, employment: {{employmentStability}}, requested amount: {{loanToIncomeRatio}}

nodes.label: Approve — With Conditions
nodes.description: Medium-risk applicant: approve with mitigating conditions
nodes.systemPrompt: You are a credit decisioning system. Generate a conditional credit approval for a near-prime applicant. Include: approved amount (may be reduced), interest rate range (above prime), specific conditions (additional documentation, collateral requirements, co-signer, lower limit), and compliance disclosures.
nodes.template: Generate conditional credit approval for applicant type: {{applicantType}}, credit score: {{creditScoreRange}}, DTI: {{dtiRatio}}, collateral: {{hasCollateral}}, employment: {{employmentStability}}, amount class: {{loanToIncomeRatio}}. Identify specific risk mitigants required.

nodes.label: Approve — Subprime Rate
nodes.description: Higher-risk applicant: approve at subprime rate with strict terms
nodes.systemPrompt: You are a credit decisioning system. Generate a subprime credit approval with risk-appropriate terms. Include: reduced approved amount, higher interest rate range, mandatory collateral or co-signer requirements, shorter review periods, and full adverse action disclosures per ECOA if terms differ significantly from requested.
nodes.template: Generate subprime credit terms for applicant type: {{applicantType}}, credit score: {{creditScoreRange}}, DTI: {{dtiRatio}}, collateral: {{hasCollateral}}, employment: {{employmentStability}}, amount requested: {{loanToIncomeRatio}}. Include risk-based pricing justification.

nodes.label: Decline — Adverse Action
nodes.description: High-risk applicant: decline with required adverse action notice
nodes.systemPrompt: You are a credit decisioning system. Generate a compliant adverse action notice per ECOA and Fair Credit Reporting Act requirements. Include: specific reasons for denial (up to 4, non-discriminatory), credit bureau used, applicant's right to obtain free credit report within 60 days, right to dispute, and contact information for the creditor. Do NOT include any language that could be interpreted as discriminatory under fair lending laws.
nodes.template: Generate adverse action notice for: applicant type: {{applicantType}}, credit score: {{creditScoreRange}}, DTI: {{dtiRatio}}, collateral: {{hasCollateral}}, employment: {{employmentStability}}, requested amount: {{loanToIncomeRatio}}. Provide exactly 4 specific, compliant denial reasons.

nodes.label: Data Retention Policy
nodes.description: All decision data must be retained per regulatory requirements
nodes.assumption: All inputs, intermediate risk scores, weight configurations, and final decisions will be retained for a minimum of 25 months per ECOA record retention requirements, and 7 years for commercial credit. Decision audit trail must include the weight profile used at time of decision.
